---
title: "Git Notes"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## PAT

```{r}
#install.packages("usethis")
library(usethis)
create_github_token()



#install.packages("gitcreds")
library(gitcreds)
gitcreds_set()
```

Git keeps track of changes to your files over time and allows you to "rollback" to previous versions.

To work with git you first initialize a git repo. in Rstudio this means you tick a litte "use git" button when creating a project

On the command line git init does the same thing

Then add and edit files

When you want to store a copy of this state of your files you do 2 key steps

git add and git commit

```         
```
